countries.name: Finland
countries.notes: Nearly self-sufficient (3.8% imported 2024). Olkiluoto 3 added 1650 MW in 2023.

countries.name: Estonia
countries.notes: Imports ~38% of electricity. Narva oil shale is primary domestic generation.

countries.name: Latvia
countries.notes: ~85% self-generation from Daugava hydropower (weather-dependent). Hosts Incukalns UGS.

countries.name: Lithuania
countries.notes: Historically imported ~70% electricity (improving to ~37% in 2024). Operates Klaipeda LNG.

countries.name: Sweden
countries.notes: Source of NordBalt power to Lithuania via SE4 bidding zone.

countries.name: Poland
countries.notes: Sole synchronous AC connection to Continental Europe via LitPol Link. GIPL gas pipeline.

countries.name: Russia
countries.notes: Former BRELL partner. Electricity exports to FI cut May 2022. Gas exports ceased 2022.

countries.name: Germany
countries.notes: Connected to Finland via C-Lion1 telecom cable (not energy).

interconnectors.name: Estlink 2
interconnectors.notes: HVDC classic. Damaged Dec 2024 (Eagle S), repaired June 2025.

interconnectors.name: NordBalt
interconnectors.notes: Suspected anchor damage Jan 2025 (Yi Peng 3 incident).

interconnectors.name: LitPol Link
interconnectors.notes: Sole synchronous AC connection post-BRELL. Commercial capacity ~200-365 MW (2026), rising to 500 MW by 2027.

interconnectors.name: Estonia-Latvia AC Lines
interconnectors.notes: Three 330 kV AC lines. ~1400-1600 MW total capacity.

interconnectors.name: Balticconnector
interconnectors.notes: Damaged Oct 2023 (Newnew Polar Bear anchor), offline 6.5 months, repaired Apr 2024.

interconnectors.name: Estonia-Latvia Gas Pipeline
interconnectors.notes: Vireši-Tallinn pipeline. Main north-south gas artery.

facilities.name: Klaipėda LNG Terminal
facilities.notes: FSRU Independence. Covers ~90% of combined Baltic gas demand.

facilities.name: Inkoo FSRU
facilities.notes: FSRU Exemplar. Arrived Dec 2022. Finnish gas security anchor.

facilities.name: Inčukalns Underground Gas Storage
facilities.notes: Only functional UGS in Baltic region. Seasonal balancing for four countries.

facilities.name: Olkiluoto 3
facilities.notes: EPR reactor. Operational 2023. Largest single power unit in Finland.

facilities.name: Narva Oil Shale Plants
facilities.notes: Estonia's primary domestic generation. Oil shale-fired. Declining capacity.

planned.name: Harmony Link
planned.notes: Redesigned from offshore HVDC to overland 220kV AC. Construction tenders 2026. Crosses Suwałki Gap.

planned.name: EstLink 3
planned.notes: MoU signed June 2022 between Elering and Fingrid.

reserve_notes: Post-BRELL, Baltics must hold ~1500 MW of reserve for frequency stability.
